panels.description: Medium shot: Count Dracula's strong hand, with long, sharp nails, grasps the slender neck of a beautiful fair woman. His face is contorted in a mask of primal fury, eyes blazing blue with rage, white teeth bared, and fair cheeks blazing red with passion. The woman struggles, a look of profound fear in her wide eyes. Dramatic, high-contrast lighting emphasizes the Count's menacing features, with deep shadows in the background of a dark, gothic chamber. 'I' observe from a hidden, fearful perspective.

Dynamic medium shot, low angle: Count Dracula, mid-motion, his arm powerfully extended after having hurled the fair woman away. She is sprawled on the floor, disheveled. The Count's eyes are locked on a group of 'other women' who are recoiling in fear and subservience. His mouth is open in a fierce shout, conveying immense power and command. Heavy shadows and a tense atmosphere fill the ornate, gothic chamber.

bubbles.text: "How dare you touch him, any of you? How dare you cast eyes on him when I had forbidden it? Back, I tell you all! This man belongs to me! Beware how you meddle with him, or you'll have to deal with me."

panels.description: Medium shot, slight low angle: A defiant 'other woman' stands forward, lips curled in a sneer, addressing the Count (off-panel). Behind her, two other women join in a chilling, mirthless, hard, and soulless laughter, their faces contorted into disturbing, almost skeletal grins. The lighting is dim and oppressive, casting unsettling shadows on their expressions in the dark chamber.

bubbles.text: "You yourself never loved; you never love!"

panels.description: Medium-close shot on Count Dracula's face. His expression has shifted from fury to a chillingly composed, predatory smile. His eyes gleam with a sinister intelligence, promising something perverse and cold. Backlit by an ominous, faint glow, his sharp features and cold demeanor are emphasized, conveying absolute control and a sinister plan.

bubbles.text: "Yes, I too can love; you yourselves can tell it from the past. Is it not so? Well, now I promise you that when I am done with him you shall kiss him at your will. Now go! go! I must awaken him, for there is work to be done."

panels.description: Medium shot, from a slightly overhead angle, focusing on the floor of the gothic chamber. A dark, worn leather bag lies on the floor, subtly shifting and trembling as if something living is inside. One of the 'other women' kneels eagerly beside it, pointing with a low, knowing laugh. In the background, the Count watches with a slow, deliberate nod of his head, a sinister amusement in his eyes. Her hands reach out, anticipating opening the bag.

Sequence shot, dynamic and disorienting: First, a tight close-up on the opening of the leather bag, revealing only a glimpse of a small, dark, ambiguous shape within, accompanied by visual sound effects (like 'GASP' and 'WAIL') suggesting a half-smothered child. Immediately transitioning to a wide, distorted shot from 'I's' collapsing point of view, showing the 'other women' closing in, their forms blurring and disappearing into wisps of shadow, along with the dreadful bag. Finally, a sudden, stark cut to a wide shot of 'I's' own bed, perfectly made and empty, in a stark, ordinary room, contrasting sharply with the preceding horror, emphasizing the abrupt and unsettling awakening.
